payload.reasoning: Haaland has 28 goals this season and loves playing at the Etihad. Against Arsenal's high line, his pace and finishing will be lethal.
payload.alternative_reasoning: Saka is in hot form with a recent hat-trick, but Haaland's ceiling is higher.

payload.reasoning: Low ownership but high upside. Arsenal's captain takes set-pieces and has been delivering consistent returns.
payload.alternative_reasoning: If playing as a super-sub, Rashford could deliver explosive minutes-per-point value.

payload.reasoning: 21 assists this season. Against Newcastle's compact defense, his vision and through-balls will unlock chances.
payload.alternative_reasoning: If you need a differential, Bellingham combines goals and assists in big games.

payload.reasoning: City have the best defensive record at home. Ederson is reliable and the back four rarely concedes at the Etihad.
payload.alternative_reasoning: If you expect an open game, skip clean sheets and load up on attackers from both sides.

payload.reasoning: Arsenal have a kind run of fixtures. Saka and Odegaard should feast on lower-table teams.
payload.alternative_reasoning: If you have the funds, Haaland + Foden + De Bruyne is a template-breaking strategy.

payload.reasoning: Playing against his former club at the Bernabeu. He thrives in UCL knockouts and already scored the late winner in the first leg.
payload.alternative_reasoning: If Madrid get early counters, Vinicius's pace could destroy Dortmund on the break.
